author: @woodworks2123
text:
Great idea. As a joiner and cabinet maker I'm asked almost daily "can I do this" or "is this easy or possible to make" I'd be rich if I had a couple quid each time I was asked. 
I would like to see more people like you Robert that do and try instead or hearing or reading something someone else said and shouting this as a universal truth. I think the whole point in science is proving what was a fact yesterday is now wrong because you discovered something new. It stifles progress and people trying things. A great example is the concept of perpetual motion, Manet motors etc. Laughed at by most people spouting the laws of thermodynamics without actually knowing them. Nobody wants to even go near that field for fear of being laughed at. There are many fake videos showing such things that now if anyone actually did invent a genuine device it would automatically be called fake. I firmly believe the magnetic repulsion force exerted from strong neodymium  magnets esp large ones There is surely a way to make this work to create a spinning motor like device with the use of flywheels, gravity, or a combination of things that almost work on their own but not quite but combined there must be a way.

author: @jpsimon206
text: It's a great idea in theory, but who certifies the experts as being experts? What criteria do they use? I imagine customers would want some assurance that the individual is in fact an expert in the subject. How could you achieve this without inherently discriminating against real-world expertise, or autodidactism? The only sorting method I can come up with would be educational accolades. I don't think I'm alone in thinking that many people seeking solutions might be better served by the blue collar guy who does that work all day as opposed to his professor.

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
I find that YouTube content is about offering ideas. People can then take those ideas and tweak them to best serve their purposes.
When people ask questions that you don't have the time to answer, quite often other viewers step in to offer their suggestions, this kind of sharing community is why I think you should start a Reddit Sub, it's something that you won't have to spend much time on but where viewers can help each other with the experience they have that could benefit others in need.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
